entry.title: Re: ECC with SFIN on Hana Archiving
entry.content:
The best option is to keep in on-prem, and powered off.

Turn the system on by request when someone needs to access the data.

entry.title: Create A Mobile Attendance Application
entry.content:
Hi folks

I'm new to SAP Builder or AppGyver and will like to build a simple mobile attendance app for a small group.

The attendance app just need only local storage.  

Will need only items such as the following:

Name               Department        Status

 Michael         Sales                   Working

Jean              Marketing            MC

Mary             Production          Annuel Leave

Will like to know how to link the logic behind the buttons.

Meanwhile, any advice from you will be greatly appreciated.

Thank you with regards.

Best regards

Johnny

entry.title: Re: Insertion of extra line item in delivery creation
entry.content:
Topic is really old but people are still searching:
Solution:
CALL FUNCTION 'RV_DELIVERY_CREATE'
      EXPORTING 
....
      is_delivery_extend = ls_deliv_extend       "here put the delivery which you want to add another item

entry.title: RAP630 - Cloud ERP Extensibility - Updated using two new ADT based wizards
entry.content:
Since last SAP TechEd there was a script available that shows how to leverage developer extensibility using ABAP cloud called  RAP630 - Cloud ERP Extensibility .

abap-platform-rap630/README.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap630 (github.com)

Since meanwhile two new ADT based wizards have been released for ABAP cloud you can walk now much faster through this script and it also shows a new feature of letting you generate your custom UI on top of a C1-released RAP business object.

The first wizard lets you simply right-click on a C1-release RAP business object:

 

Create new bank

Whereas the second wizard, the new field extension wizard lets you add fields to an extensible RAP business object.

In order to let several developers test this new feature the RAP630 script contains a class that generates an extensible RAP BO for your convenience in the customer Z-namespace based on my openSource RAP Generator. 

By simply right clicking on the projection view of the released RAP business object the generator will generate all required repository objects.

 

If you want to try out this yourself either go and get a user in a SAP BTP ABAP Environment trial system. 

Create an SAP BTP ABAP Environment Trial User | SAP Tutorials

An example how to use the field extension wizard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud has been shown by the community member taranam in the following blog post:

S/4HANA Cloud: Field Extension Wizard in S/4HANA C... - SAP Community

Kind regards,   

Andre

entry.title: Utilize Requirement Modeling in Software Engineering for Functional Specification [FS]
entry.content:
Introduction

The functional specification is very crucial for requirement documentation and management when all the functional and technical SAP consultants work on SAP RICEFW during the realization of any SAP project.

In this blog post I would like to discuss about requirements engineering standard practices and requirement modeling in high level which can be utilized for Functional Specification derived from requirements documentation.

Requirements Engineering:

  • is a set of procedures used to establish the customer requirements from a system including the constraints under which it operates and is developed.
  • is a well organized approach to eliciting, organizing, and documenting the requirements of the system, and a set of procedures that formulates and maintains agreement between the customer and the project team on the changing requirements of the system.

Requirements are:

  • Statements of needs from a expected system.
  • System features and constraints characterization that are identified while the requirements elicitation.

Requirements Activities:

Following diagram depicted activities of requirement engineering process in a high level.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Requirements Types:

Requirements can be classified under following ways.

  • Functional requirements
    • Basic subject matter how behave the system and are measured by concrete means such as data values, logic for decision making and algorithms.
  • Non-functional requirements
    • Behavioral properties that the required functions must have, such as performance and scalability, reliability, etc.
  • Project constraints
    • Recognize the ways of final product must suitable into the world.
    • Integration with some existing hardware, software or business practice.
    • Defined project scope.
  • Project drivers
    • Business- related forces.
    • Purpose of the product is a project driver, as are all of the stakeholders.
  • Project issues
    • Define the conditions under which the project will be done.
    • Included to present a coherent picture of all factors contributing to the success or failure.

IEEE 830 Documentation Standard for a Software Requirements Specification (1998):

Criteria for a high-quality requirements set are:

  • Unambiguous: Every statement has unique interpretation. Terms should be clear and well defined.
  • Complete: All major requirements are considered. No items have been disregarded for future definition.
  • Verifiable: All functionalities defined as part of the project have a corresponding test to determine whether they have been successfully delivered.
  • Consistent: Final product's end users benefit by getting a product that is easy to use enhancing the user experience.
  • Modifiable: The cost of change and refers to the ease with which a software system can accommodate changes.
  • Traceable: Uniquely identification is required for each referenced requirement.
  • Correct: Every stated requirement represents something required of the system to be built.

Requirements Modelling:

Unified Modeling Language (UML)  and Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN) can be used for requirement modeling with graphical representation by diagrams. You can freely use draw.io online application to prepare diagrams.

  • Functional Requirements
    • Hierarchy
    • Data Flow Diagrams
    • Use Cases
    • Activity Diagrams
    • Other graphical models
  • Data Requirements
    • Entity relationship diagram
  • Linking data and functional requirements
  • Maintaining the link between textual and graphical requirements documents

Benefits of Models:

  • Provide a visual, non-technical representation
  • Are easy to understand
  • Quick to produce and easy to amend
  • Use limited number of symbols with specific meanings
    • implicit presentation of information
    • concise system descriptions
    • enable code generation through CASE
  • Minimize redundancy of information
    • effects of changes are easily identified and incorporated
  • Use a simple, top-down expansion
    • easy progression between levels of detail
    • convenient review of the overall system structure

Good Requirements Guidelines:

  • Add simple direct sentences
    • As short as possible but no shorter
  • Add a limited vocabulary
    • Project glossary also recommend
  • Focus on results (goal)
  • Define verifiable criteria
    • later relate to Acceptance Criterion

In conclusion, with above blog post your are able to get an overview idea of requirement engineering paradigm and possible opportunities to utilize them with sap RICEFW requirement modeling.

Hope you have gained something it and stay tuned for real world scenario with simple examples in my future blog.

entry.title: Simulation start and end pages do not appear on Companion
entry.content:
I have used one of the SAP Simulation Templates to with Start and end pages. When I preview in Producer and Manager, it starts with Start page and ends with end page.

 

However, when I publish this on Companion, the start and end page do not appear.

 

Could you please help.

entry.title: Re: Price items in Service Transactions
entry.content:
Hi Jane, this feature is only available with Item-based accounting (the new Finance integration for Service Contracts and Service Orders). Item-based Accounting only supports Event-based Revenue recognition (EBRR). 

Regards, Gert

entry.title: Re: MDF Multiple Changes Per Day_Boomi taking only latest record for integration
entry.content: Hey @jaisonpj now I know it has been quite some time, but have you found the solution to this issue?

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana Public Cloud - Depreciation areas for assets
entry.content:
Thanks for putting in the solution you found yourself 🙂

For customer defined Asset Classes you need to add the Valuation Areas to the new Asset Class. The below link contains all the AA customizing points for reference. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/227f0f8f970a44f88d6c1b875971c306.html?locale=en-US

Make Valuation View-Dependent Settings for Asset Class (102609)

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana Public Cloud - Depreciation areas for assets
entry.content: Also for reference the error is AA025 No depreciation areas have been defined for asset &-&

entry.title: How to change the value of a parameter used in several selection screens
entry.content:
The below program has 2 selection screens 1000 and 1001, both having the same parameter DUMMY, and we go from 1000 to 1001 by pressing F8 (Execute), and back to 1000 by pressing F3 (Back).

If I type a value in the parameter DUMMY in the selection screen 1001, press Enter, go back to the selection screen 1000, this value is not shown in the selection screen 1000, and by debug we can see that the global variable DUMMY has been cleared by the system.

How to retain the parameter value?

For information, if I type the value in the selection screen 1000 and go to the selection screen 1001, the value is shown. It seems to be a backward-only "issue".

REPORT zzsro_selection_screen_include.

TABLES sscrfields.

PARAMETERS dummy.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 1001.
  SELECTION-SCREEN COMMENT /1(83) text1001.
  SELECTION-SCREEN INCLUDE PARAMETERS dummy.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 1001.

INITIALIZATION.
  text1001 = 'Screen 1001'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF sy-dynnr = '1000' AND sscrfields-ucomm = 'ONLI'.
    sscrfields-ucomm = ''.
    CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 1001.
  ENDIF.
  IF sy-dynnr = '1001' AND sscrfields-ucomm = 'CRET'.
    sscrfields-ucomm = ''. " Execute -> stays on 1001
  ENDIF.

entry.title: Getting 500 Error when Trying to execute POST request in Automation Pilot
entry.content:
I'm trying to use the HttpRequest to run an Incentive Management Pipeline using POST request.  The command uses the clientId, clientSecret, body with the parameters needed, and the tokenUrl.

I can run the request in Postman successfully but in Automation Pilot, I'm getting a 500 (Internal Server Error) response.

When I run a GET request, such as searching for a specific period, it completes successfully.  Not with the POST request.

How can I figure out what's causing this error so that I can fix it?  Any and all suggestions are welcome.  😊

These APIs are in GCP.

Thanks in advance!

Ron

entry.title: BPA for ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am looking to create/configure a bot in BPA for SAP Business ByDesign.

This bot has to locate the projects (KUT extension is added to projects of type INDICATOR) that are ready to be assigned with a team on the Header. The team information is available on an EXCEL sheet (placed in an FTP/Sharepoint site).

Can I develop/configure such a bot in BPA to connect to ByDesign, Retrieve the Projects, and then connect to Sharepoint to access the file assign the team, and offset the indicator on the projects automatically in one shot?

I also want to know how to trigger the bot, I mean which of the 3 types would be the appropriate one to trigger the process in the BPA so the bot can do its job?

#SAPBusinessByDesign #ByDesign #BPA #SAPBPA #SAPByD #BTP

entry.title: Re: How to change the value of a parameter used in severa...
entry.content:
When leaving a selection screen, the parameters are saved by the kernel to the memory, and when coming back to the selection screen, they are restored from the memory.

Add the below code, that will replace the values in memory with the current parameter values, when leaving the selection screen 1001 to get back to the selection screen 1000. That's an "evil" workaround and may stop working at any time if SAP does a change of the "kernel" or any system ABAP program (RSDBSPVA, RSDBRUNT, etc.) so better redesign your program to use any other supported solution.

 

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON EXIT-COMMAND.
  IF sy-dynnr = '1001'.
    PERFORM export_variables.
  ENDIF.

FORM export_variables.
  FIELD-SYMBOLS <restore_memkey> TYPE RSVAMEMKEY.
  ASSIGN ('(RSDBSPVA)RESTORE-MEMKEY') TO <restore_memkey>.
  PERFORM %_export_var_to_mem IN PROGRAM (sy-repid) USING <restore_memkey>.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: Payroll Posting to Account with Business Partner
entry.content:
Dear Experts:

We are facing the issue during payroll posting to account that multiple employees are getting posted to single Business Partner whereas each employee has their respective Business partner created in system with same employee number.

Screen short is given below to elaborate the issue, showing that employees’ numbers 10000145 and 10000146 are mapped against their own business partner, whereas the loan amount of both employees are posted to 10000145 only.

entry.title: Re: Planned order created from sales order even if there...
entry.content:
Hello

Very often this is an issue with your rescheduling check. Either production orders are not included in the rescheduling check, of this specific order is outside the rescheduling horizon. 

Check this WIKI for more details about the rescheduling check:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/mrp/3138697863.html

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Payroll Posting to Account with Business Partner
entry.content: Dear Tariq Please ensure symbolic account are configure properly,

entry.title: Re: I am unable post deferred tax transfer documents in T...
entry.content:
Hi sir, hope you are well. 

The solution for this problem is in the Tax codes, you need to review the configuration of the tax rates that are in the tax codes. 

For example I use the tax code 2C / 16% but also when I execute the deferred tax transfer it points to the 3C / 16% tax code.

You need to verify that the tax rate is maintained in both tax codes.

Best Regards. 

-Michel Garcia.

entry.title: Re: Digital Discovery Assessment - The guided decision between SAP S/4HANA Public or Private Cloud E
entry.content:
Hello dears,

Can you please help me answering this question? Thank you very much!

  1. Which information does the SAP Digital Discovery Assessment provide? Note: There are 2 correct answers to this question.
  • A. Localizations and legal entities
  • B. Draft project schedule and roadmap
  • C. List of recommended scope items
  • D. Known integration requirements

entry.title: Re: Consume HANA views in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Thank you. Even i was thinking why 2 warehouses. Your comments clarify. Thank you.

entry.title: More Enhancements for SAP Community
entry.content:
Although it's been more than a month since our last announcement about enhancements (meaning improvements and bug fixes), I'm happy to report that we recently rolled out some fairly significant updates to the platform -- including perhaps the thing most demanded by members. Specifically…

Additional Sorting Options for SAP Managed Tags

Since the migration to the new platform, members have been asking for a way to sort SAP Managed Tag feeds by content type (e.g., blog posts vs. Q&A). Now members can do that and more -- sorting by all types of posts available within SAP Community, as well as by creation date, activity (e.g., most commented), and area within the platform (e.g., a specific Q&A board).

See for yourself. Visit any SAP Managed Tag page and try out the various options until you're able to find the content that most appeals to you.

More Prominent Placement of SAP Managed Tags

Another adjustment related to SAP Managed Tags: They now appear at the top of a post (e.g., question, blog), so readers can identify immediately what the content is about.

In the previous layout, the tags appeared at the end of the post, and members commented that they'd like this information sooner. Now they get it right away -- at the beginning of the post.

Better Log-In Experience

If you are one of the members who complained about the constant need to log in each and every time you visited us here, then you may have already noticed this change.

With this improvement, when you log in, you stay logged in for 30 days -- as long as you don't log out/clear your cache.

This improvement goes across platforms, so if you log in to SAP.com, support.sap.com, or learning.sap.com, you'll log in automatically when navigating to SAP Community. The same applies if you log in to SAP Community before going to SAP.com.

Group Curators Easier to Identify

If you visit a lot of areas in SAP Community, you may have thought that my colleagues and I ran just about every group -- because our names often appeared next to "Owned by" on every landing page.

In actuality, due to the way that the platform was set up, the person who created the group would appear as its primary owner, and since group creation is one of my team's responsibilities, my colleagues and I would show up as group owners -- but it's really the curators who oversee the groups once we set them up.

This approach made things a bit confusing for visitors who wanted to connect with the members who were actually managing a group. Not surprisingly, they'd end up contacting my colleagues and me.

Now each group shows its curators as the owners, so visitors can easily identify the members who kindly volunteer to run groups for specific locations, interests, and so on.  (I mean, sure, I'd love to talk to you about the awesome Melbourne group, but you're better off reaching out to my friend @pjcools, who is doing the work there. So it's better you see his name as the group owner now instead of mine!)

Clearer Options for Blog Boards

As explained in this post, our FAQ, the how-to videos, and our official SAP Community tutorial, bloggers who want to share content in the Products and Technology areas need to select between either "by Members" or "by SAP" boards (depending on whether they work for SAP). Still, it was not uncommon for bloggers to select the wrong board -- only to be greeted by this error message as a result:

Now, to avoid that experience, the Create a Blog button will be grayed out when bloggers attempt to post content in a board that isn't open to their submissions. For example, as an SAP employee, I'll see this if I try to blog in the CRM and CX Blogs by Members board (because I should be posting in the CRM and CX Blogs by SAP board instead):

With this change, we hope our bloggers will be able to locate the correct boards more easily when they wish to share their knowledge with other community members.

Bullet Bug Be Gone!

I'll wrap up with a fix for something that had been bugging members.

Members had reported that when they added a nested bullet list within a bullet list, the system would automatically add an extra space. That's been fixed:

While that fix may not seem like a major thing, I know this bug had been aggravating people during the editing process, so I'm glad to pass along this news.

I'll be back later this week with an SAP Community Voice newsletter highlighting new and popular content. And as more improvements and fixes get introduced, I'll be reporting them here in What's New as well. So remember to subscribe to this board and keep an eye out for the latest announcements.

entry.title: Re: Actual dates & durations in AFC
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: Issues with an INT64 parameter in a Com UFL..
entry.content: Thank you for the feedback. Of course I simplified my question as much as possible to focus on the root cause of my issue: Why can't I have an INT64 parameter in UFL function? Nonetheless, your suggestion of a SQL Expression for my TOSTRING(table.column) is a good one. I can reference that in my formula and it works great a runtime. The Designer though (I'm using CR 2016 at the moment) insists on doing a select TOSTRING(table.column) every time I look at or modify that SQL Expression. There are millions of rows in my table and this takes forever. Is there any way to suppress the design from doing that? I can't put a where clause on the SQL Expression because the real clause for the SQL is created at runtime.

entry.title: Re: Actual dates & durations in AFC
entry.content:
Any suggestion on this topic ?

This is for me a basic requirement which was possible in FCC but I cannot find an equivalent in AFC, nor any plan in the product roadmap.

entry.title: Re: GENERATIVE AI HUB SDK - Python Error
entry.content:
hi @lnog 

so happy to see progress!

Could be the case that your Langchain library got an update, and I might have provided also old instructions, but first make sure you update LangChain Version.

 
pip install --upgrade langchain
 
Instead of using the deprecated LLMChain class, the warning suggests using RunnableSequence. 1. Then lets replace the LLMChain usage with the suggested alternative.
 
# Old deprecated usage
chain = LLMChain(prompt=prompt, llm=llm)
# New recommended usage
chain = prompt | llm
 
2. Instead of using the deprecated Chain.run method, the warning recommends using invoke.
# Old deprecated usage
output = chain.run(input_data)
# New recommended usage
output = chain.invoke(input_data)
 
Let me know how it goes

entry.content: Refer to documentation "Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS)","Data Transfer to Project System (PS)" and Confirmation via Time Sheets (CATS).

entry.title: Cross-company Strock transfer order with Advanced return management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Most of the large companies run SAP and some data shows that 75% of world’s transaction happen in SAP. Most of these companies operate in several countries and there are transfer of their products from one country to another. This transfer of materials can occur through

  • classic intercompany or advanced intercompany stock transfer
  • advanced intercompany sales and advanced intercompany stock transfer

as shown in below image.

However, there may be situations that receiving company wants to return the received materials to the delivering company for different reasons like poor quality, over-delivery etc. SAP S/4HANA provides the option of cross-company stock transfer order to return the material to the delivering company using Advanced return management (ARM) as shown in below image.

Process starts with a

  1. Return cross-company stock transfer order with a flag on ‘Return item’

2. Outbound delivery is created from the return STO.

3. Inbound delivery is created from SPED output of the outbound delivery

4. Goods receipt happens with movement type 673 and stock moves to Quality inspection

5. Inspection certifies the material as OK

6. Stock is moved to Free available stock as Logistic follow up activity

7. Credit memo is created with respect to the inbound delivery

ARM also provides a Return overview with all the relevant documents and their statuses.

ARM provides an integrated and transparent return with automatic logistical follow up activities for all possible return scenarios.ARM also works well with EWM managed warehouses.

This blog is based on my personal insights, observation, and tests in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 release in my preparation for Workshop on Reverse Logistics for Advanced intercompany flows for one of my projects. Will appreciate your views / comment and feedback.

entry.title: Re: Sessions TechEd Virtual VS Live

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.14
entry.content:
@meganhoy The help file for the "apply advanced filters" section has a discrepancy between the filter definition section (2 quarters of actuals and 2 quarters of forecast) and the rest of the examples (1 quarter of actuals, 3 quarters of forecast)

Would it be possible to align either the table screenshots or the definition screenshot? It is a little confusing as it is right now. (If I am even understanding it correctly)

Apply Advanced Filters and Hide Unbooked Members to Create Table Layouts for Ad-Hoc Planning (Optimized) | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Explore the Newly Redesigned Cloud Card Tab!
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the newly redesigned Cloud tab in SAP for Me’s Finance & Legal Dashboard, now providing a single, comprehensive view of a customer’s | partner’s Cloud Contracts. This update combines the previous individual cards (Cloud Materials, Cloud Orders, and Cloud Contracts) into one streamlined view, making it easy for users to find all the details relating to the management of their Cloud contracts.

Key Features of the New Cloud Contracts Card:

Unified View: The Contracts card under the Cloud tab now consolidates all Customer | Partner cloud contracts, providing visibility to expiring contracts at the top, sorted by end date.

Retained Functionalities: Enjoy the same popular features such as search functionality, contract expiry notifications, and direct access to contract details.

Enhanced Capabilities: Users can customize their view by filtering, sorting, grouping, and adjusting columns as per their needs. 

New Cloud Card in SAP for Me

Important Note: The "Manage Contract" options depend on the contract status, so not all options may be available to every customer at all times. For any questions, please contact your SAP Representative.

For further details on navigating the new Cloud Contracts card, including how to access and utilize its features, please refer to the following resources:

  • My Renewals @ SAP for Me Customer Reference Guide: Comprehensive information about the new Cloud tab as well as access, authorizations, purchase order submission, cloud contract functionality, and more.
  • SAP for Me Online Help

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow for Maintenance in S/4 HANA
entry.content:

In the Manage Work flow for Asset Management / maintenance order app, I have already set up a workflow rule so that if the planned cost is greater than $1000, it goes through an approval.

 

The maintenance order does enter authorization with the ORAI Order Approval In Process status. I look for the workflow in the inbox and it doesn't arrive.

 

My question is where can I approve the maintenance order?

Thank you for your response.

entry.title: Where to find SAP wide Forum for AI SAP specific topics?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

currently, we are working on an AI tool and I noticed that there are a ton of design decisions to be made and therefore a lot of research is necessary. 

My high level question is: Is there a platform for AI Developers coming from all the different LOBs to exchange information, asking questions about SAP specifics and learn from each other?

Is this blog/discussion the right place? Even if it is open for non-SAP readers?

Happy to hear your thoughts!

Max

entry.title: Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content:
Hello @kmanur 

I'm not sure what you're asking exactly. Are you getting an error when trying to access either of the following URLs?

  • https://localhost:4200/electronics-spa/en/USD
  • https://localhost:4200/apparel-uk-spa/en/GBP

If so, what error messages are you getting? The error message you posted relates to an occ endpoint, not the storefront URLs.

entry.title: Re: NFe Cloud Outbound - Outbound Invoicing Option for Brazil - Como funciona?
entry.content:
Oi, Secco! Tudo bem?

O envio do e-mail no processo de outbound é feito pelo DRC, correto?

Sabe se tem como trocar a mensagem do corpo do e-mail? Acredito que não, mas só para confirmar.

Pelo que vi as informações que geram o e-mail estão no JSON, com isto o DRC recebe, envia o XML para a SEFAZ e o e-mail com base nesta informação no JSON.

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: @akhilgeo it is so great to meet you! Welcome! I live in the Philadelphia USA. May I ask where you live? There are many location groups where you may connect with SAPers in your proximity.

entry.title: SAP BRIM SOM - Unable to display custom Sub Order type in the create order pop up
entry.content:
Hi SAP BRIM SOM Experts,

I am new to SAP BRIM and recently started learning. I am trying to create a new custom Subscription Order type 'ZPRO' under Transaction Type and assigned Leading Transaction Category = Subscription Order. Basically copied from standard Subscription Order PRVO.

At New Subcription order creation, pop up shows Standard as well as few other custom Sub Order transaction type to choose, but doesn't show the 'ZPRO' in the list. All the config of the Transaction type both standard, other custom and this newly created 'ZPRO' is same.

Am I missing anything to configure? Appreciate the help.

SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management, subscription order management SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Welcome!

entry.title: Which Technology SSO implement
entry.content:
Hello All

We are in the process of integrating Single Sign-On (SSO) into our S/4Hana system, version 2020 initial stack.

Our user base is diverse, comprising individuals with and without email addresses. also, the Vendor users outside our domain. and these users interact with our system through both the GUI and the Fiori web interface.

all of the above users use Windows and Mac systems 

so we need your opinion on what the optimal choice technology should be used for SAP Single Sign-On utilizing Kerberos/SPNEGO or X.509 certificates

if there is some installation guide to implement the SSO

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: @SabinePruse it is great to meet you! As a next step do you want to join the Welcome to the beginner corner! - SAP Community and start a discussion there with others! I bet you'll get a lot of ideas and some great networking to boot! Best of luck!

entry.title: Which Technology SSO implement
entry.content:
Hello All

We are in the process of integrating Single Sign-On (SSO) into our S/4Hana system, version 2020 initial stack.

Our user base is diverse, comprising individuals with and without email addresses. also, the Vendor users outside our domain. and these users interact with our system through both the GUI and the Fiori web interface.

all of the above users use Windows and Mac systems 

so we need your opinion on what the optimal choice technology should be used for SAP Single Sign-On utilizing Kerberos/SPNEGO or X.509 certificates

if there is some installation guide to implement the SSO

entry.title: Re: Header level Aggregated value ZPR1, needs to automati...
entry.content:
Hi Abul,

In you pricing procedure, in the step where you have ZPMO, put in the FROM cell the value of the step where ZPR1 is determined. 

Please see the below standard example for reference. In this case, condition type DRG1 uses the value from step 100 (Gross Value) and it applies against it the percentage entered.

Regards,

-Carlos

entry.title: Re: Modelo de Autorización con Condiciones basadas en con...

Hola buen dia comidad, tengo una duda sobre un requermiento.

en el modulo de venta ->factura a deudores, necesito una autorizacion que cuando el usaurio quiera camabiar el precio del articulo me pida la autorizacion de un usaurio con permisos mayore. he estado investigando sobre el asunto ya que en el modulo de autorizaciones no hay autorizacion que me permita dicho requerimiento,  estoy investigando los modelos de autorizaciones basados en Querys pero aun no tengo nada  concreto.

De antemano gracias.

pendiente  a sus respuestas.

entry.title: Re: Formating SO10 in Adobe with SmartStyle.
entry.content: This does not work. I created a style ZCM_BOLD and added character format for bold characters. I only want certain characters from the SO10 text to be bold but when I apply the above properties the entire thing is bold. If I try to use the character format BD that I created in the text via <BD></> it just displays the <BD></> and doesn't apply the character format just to the words I want it to. This should not be that complicated and it's frustrating that SAP requires jumping through this many hoops just to get a simple formatting thing to work!!!!

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: Here's My Solution.

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
My solution:

 

 

 

CLASS zms_cl_travel_copy DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS zms_cl_travel_copy IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    DATA: lt_travel TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zms_db_travel.

    SELECT client, travel_id, description, total_price, currency_code,
    CASE
      WHEN status = 'N' THEN 'O'
      WHEN status = 'P' THEN 'O'
      WHEN status = 'B' THEN 'A'
      ELSE 'X'
      END AS status
    FROM /dmo/travel
    INTO TABLE _travel.
    IF lt_travel IS NOT INITIAL.
      "Refresh the existing data if any.
      DELETE zms_db_travel FROM TABLE _travel.
      "Populate the new data.
      INSERT zms_db_travel FROM TABLE _travel.
      IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
        DATA(lv_message) = |'Data inserted successfully!'- { sy-dbcnt }|.
        out->write( lv_message ).
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: SO10 Text to print on adobe using SE72?
entry.content: Doing this makes the entire text bold. What if I only want a few words of the text in bold. Nothing I have tried is working. Tried creating character format inside of style for bold characters using "BA" and even if I include <BA></> in the text it still makes the entire thing bold and displays the hypertext params.

entry.title: Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content:
Hello Pierre,

Thanks so much for responding.

Yes -- when i hit the http://localhost:4200/electronics-spa/en/USD ( i believe it is http and not https)

I see that ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR on the browser side. It most likely seems like CORS kind of an error. I have tried to follow exactly the steps mentioned above. I can't seem to figure out what seems to be the problem or if any one else has bumped into issue like this before?

The following error is what i see from the console.

An unknown http error occurred Http failure response for https://localhost:9002/occ/v2/basesites?fields=FULL: 0 undefined

As i said before I can hit the OCC end point from swagger.  So i was hoping to get any suggestions as to what else I can try to get this fixed. 

-thanks,

Kiran

entry.title: Re: Sales Commissions - External Sales Representative (2TT)
entry.content:
Hi Vivek,

It is not possible to define New Condition Contract types, only limited changes can be made to the existing ones. See below.

-Carlos

entry.title: Re: HANA Express Edition-SAP Control Request failed
entry.content:
On your laptop, check that you can successfully ping to the machine running SAP HANA express.  For example, ping hxehost. 

If it is a Windows machine, you can update your hosts file.
127.0.0.1 localhost hxehost

The following might be used to verify that the database is started and reachable.
Switch to the hxeadm user
sudo su hxeadm

Attempt to connect to the server using hdbsql which should be located at /usr/sap/HXE/HDB90/exe/hdbsql

hdbsql -n hxehost:39013 -u SYSTEM -p YourHanaPWD1234


If that is successful, you can query to see the details of the port used by the SQL Port used by the tenant database using:
SELECT * FROM SYS_DATABASES.M_SERVICES WHERE SQL_PORT != 0;

entry.title: Re: Can Story custom date filter be controlled by scripts?
entry.content: I'm not sure exactly why your proposed solution won't work for PM8, but it won't work for me because your solution uses a standard input control. What I think PM8 is looking for (me as well) is a solution that uses the Current Date Input Control that gets created using a date selection in a Restricted Measure. Typically I use these to specify a date (fiscal year for example) in 1 column, and then provide an offset in subsequent columns. I have not found a solution for this scenario.

entry.title: Re: SAP provided official document type list
entry.content:
I found this link and I hope it will help you.

Default Settings for Users | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content:
Hi @kmanur,

I have not seen this issue before. I suggest you try accessing the site using https instead of http. If that doesn't help, then I suggest making sure the cors-related properties you copied to your gradle file correctly appear in your config/local.properties file. Unfortunately, apart from those two suggestions, I can't think of anything else.

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: My result:

entry.title: Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Expense
entry.content:
SAP Concur is imagining a world where travel and expenses practically manage themselves, helping businesses run at their best every day. Data Delivery Service lets you combine SAP Concur data with data from your company’s existing financial systems to see all expense data in one place. Bring data directly into your own BI tools. 

  • Control spending before it hits your budget 
  • Create, adjust, and enforce spending policies 
  • Reimburse employees more quickly 
  • Negotiate with vendors to cut costs 

In today’s data driven, analytical business environment, companies need insights that give stakeholders realizable value. To ensure our customers have the most efficient data integration, we have created an efficient, optimized, and strategic Concur Data Templates for Data Delivery Service Expense solution. We’ve created a dataflow already formatted to Concur Expense specific entries and headers so you can analyze your data and discover insights, focusing on what matters most.​ Discover new insights, trends, and predictions through powerful analytics based on cross data models. Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service (Expense) includes numerous topics related to the Expense module of SAP Concur namely, Expense Administration, Credit Card Program Management, Policy Compliance, Risk and Compliance, System Overview and the Standard stories that were previously available using the standard connector to integrate the two tools. ​

  • Accurate, Timely Reports Take the risk out of outdated reporting and be certain you’re getting the right data right when you need it, so you can count on the story it’s telling.​
  • Corporate Cards vs. Cash Watch for cash spending in categories where corporate cards give you better visibility, control, and negotiating power – such as airfare, hotels, and car rentals. ​
  • Policy Compliance Solve multiple use cases by combining some of the most requested expense policy exception metrics in one highly functional dashboard containing multiple interactive visualizations that can be dynamically filtered to answer key questions.​

By partnering with Datasphere customers have direct access to analytical data sets and analysis models; multi-dimensional modeling with powerful analytical capabilities and built-in data preview​, a graphical impact and lineage analysis to visualize data movements, transformations, and other dependencies. By consolidating plan and actual data within SAP Datasphere, organizations can foster a single source of truth, minimizing discrepancies and ensuring data integrity​. The new data templates for SAP Analytics Cloud will make it easy for SAP customers to ingest SAP Concur data to SAP Datasphere and analyze the data using templates available in SAP Analytics Cloud. This content package comprises six comprehensive reporting stories, each built from analytical models delivered through a live data connection from SAP Datasphere. 

  • SAP Travel & Expense: Expense Administration This story offers a comprehensive analysis of expenditure, outstanding spend, and workflow efficiency. By integrating and visualizing spending data alongside outstanding balances, it provides insights into financial transactions and workflow performance. 
  • SAP Travel & Expense: Credit Card Program Management Designed to streamline and oversee credit card programs, this story integrates and visualizes card usage data, providing a holistic view of cardholder activity and program efficiency. Additionally, it offers detailed insights into credit card transactions, highlighting spending patterns, transaction volumes, and potential anomalies, thereby enhancing financial oversight and fraud detection. 
  • SAP Travel & Expense: Policy Compliance This story focuses on identifying and managing expense exceptions to ensure policy compliance. Through visualizations and insights, it enables proactive identification and resolution of policy violations, enhancing financial governance and compliance measures. 
  • SAP Travel & Expense: Risk and Compliance This story provides a comprehensive overview of risk and compliance factors, including potential duplicate expenses, duplicate attendees, and credit card late fees. By analyzing expense and transaction data, it identifies potential risks and compliance issues, such as duplicate entries and late payment penalties. 
  • SAP Travel & Expense: System Management This story offers a comprehensive view of system management aspects, encompassing active user statistics, user distribution insights, and detailed employee profiles. By visualizing system usage and user demographics, it provides administrators with valuable insights into system performance and user engagement. 
  • SAP Travel & Expense: Spend Overview This story is the bridge from our previous solution with the standard connector to the new one. It covers key topics like expenses, entries per reports, reports, and Process Runtime. Designed for easy access and familiarity, it ensures you find the same data effortlessly, making your transition smooth and efficient. 

Product Content URLs

  • Content Package Announcement
  • SAP Concur Expense DDS Integration with SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Datasphere Business Content
  • SAP Analytics Cloud Content

To learn more, reach-out to your Concur account executive. Data and Analytics SAP Concur

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Hello,

Here is my second week submission.

entry.title: Re: I am unable post deferred tax transfer documents in T...
entry.content:
Hi Svanka2,

This process can fail because of multiple reasons. So you will need to check the following settings in your system and process to see if one of these apply to you.

Failure because of Tax GL account setting:

  1. Deferred tax accounts should be set up as open item management account. The SAP standard program creates a post with transfer entry which clears the items in the deferred tax account and enter the amount with a true tax into the target account.
  2. SAP also mentions in OSS note 1859099 that if the deferred accounts which will  include amount <0 then "tax category field in the GL master" should be "*" . If the accounts will have only amounts which are > 0 then "tax category" field should be created as "< - input tax"
  3. Target account (the tax receiving account) was created as open item. True tax accounts should never be created as open items. Make sure this is not the case.

When you executed the deferred program you can do it in 2 ways.

  1. Create a batch input session. With this option you can then run the batch input session in foreground and there you can exactly see what is causing the error. Here there could be multiple reasons some can be. No profit center assign to the transaction, A field for the posting was not set as "optional" and is needed for the transaction. here you will need to find out which is the cause.
  2. If you run it with direct input, then the error should be in the logs. If the logs are not descriptive enough I suggest you run it using the batch input option and see what is the error.

I hope this helps you.

happy hunting.

entry.title: Re: MDK: Problem accessing the value of a field
entry.content:
Assuming the default binding points to the record for reference you would just directly access the binding for the Activo property using:

let activo = clientAPI.binding.Activo;

entry.title: Re: HOW TO COPY OLD P.O TO CREATE NEW P.O ?
entry.content: 1-Run Tcode-ME21N 2-Select the Doc. Type 3-Click on document overview ON 4-from Document Overview Click on Purchase Order 5-A new screen will come where you put the required PO no from which you want to copy. 6-Now the PO no with Line item will copy on document overview screen 7-Select the appropriate Line item of that Po and then click on "Adopt" or if you want to copy all the line items of that particular Po then just select the Po no and then click on Adopt. 8-Now it will copy all the contents of the Copied PO into the new PO as per adoption.

entry.title: SAP Configuration to Limit the Conversion of Planned Orders into Production by Quantity
entry.content:
Estou tendo problemas para limitar a conversão da ordem de produção usando uma ordem planejada. Existe uma configuração padrão para essa limitação?

Exemplo: há uma ordem planejada para 1.000 peças. A ideia é que a conversão da ordem de produção seja limitada a 1.000 peças ou, no máximo, limite um percentual excedente. Se a limitação for definida como 5%, a ordem de produção poderá ter no máximo 1.050 peças.

Onde posso configurar essa funcionalidade sem precisar personalizá-la?

entry.title: Re: The calling AMDP method must also allow this restriction
entry.content:
Hi Dhruv,

 

Was your issue resolved?

entry.title: Re: Enhance your maintenance and field technicians’ efficiency and productivity with SAP Dynamic For
entry.content:
Support of workflows is included in the roadmap for future releases. To better understand this request of extending Dynamic Forms using SAP Build please submit an enhancement request and our product teams will asses technical feasibility. 

 

Thanks

Alba

entry.title: Re: MDK: Problem accessing the value of a field
entry.content: Hi bill ! Thanks for your help. I think I have another problem with this property... This property was added after creating the MDK application. When I add it, BAS does not show me the property. So I went to the "Services" > Auditoria.service" folder and selected the "Sync Service" option and "Activo" started to appear among the available options. But now I am working on editing the entity, and when I want to save the changes the error I am receiving is the following: "Error: "com.sap.cloud.mobile.o.data.core.UndefinitedException: Property 'Activo' not found in entity type 'InfraestructuraService.Auditoria'". I don't know why it tells me this, look at the file's metadata and it appears defined. Do I need to do some other type of update?

entry.title: Re: GL Account Write-Up in S/4 HANA
entry.content: Any thoughts on why SAP  set the Post Automatically Only filed "No" for AP reconciliation G/L accounts in SAP S/4 Cloud?

entry.title: Re: Power Designer Repository - SQL QUERY and repository...
entry.content: Hello Ondrej,
